executable.title: Gradient Anisotropic Diffusion Filter
executable.description: Applies gradient-based anisotropic diffusion to a 3D NIFTI image. Smooths regions of uniform intensity while preserving edges where the gradient magnitude is high. Controlled by conductance, timeStep, and iterations. ECE:5490 HW04 — iampartho
executable.acknowledgements: Developed as part of ECE:5490 Medical Image Analysis, Spring 2026, University of Iowa. Course instructor: Hans J. Johnson. This work uses the Insight Toolkit (ITK), an open-source library for medical image analysis developed by the National Library of Medicine (NLM) and the Insight Software Consortium (ISC). Slicer Execution Model (SEM) developed by Kitware Inc. and the Slicer community (slicer.org). Gradient anisotropic diffusion based on the Perona-Malik diffusion model: P. Perona and J. Malik, "Scale-space and edge detection using anisotropic diffusion," IEEE TPAMI, 1990. Described in Chapter 2 of the ITK Software Guide, Book 2: Design and Functionality.

double.description: Conductance parameter. Higher values allow more diffusion across edges. Typical range: 0.5–5.0.
double.label: Conductance

double.description: Time step per iteration. Must be small enough for numerical stability (typically 0.0625 for 3D).
double.label: Time Step

integer.description: Number of diffusion iterations. More iterations produce stronger smoothing.
integer.label: Iterations

image.label: Output Volume
image.description: Gradient anisotropic diffusion filtered output NIFTI image.
